title: Smoky Middle Eastern Eggs Skillet
description: A buttery, dense, and flavorful Middle Eastern-inspired dish featuring smoky eggs, perfect for a brunch mood. This FODMAP-friendly recipe combines the richness of eggs with the bold flavors of the region.
instructions: In a large oven-safe skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the smoked paprika, sumac, salt, and black pepper, and stir for 1 minute.
Crack in the eggs and scramble them gently until they start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked to your desired doneness.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the chopped parsley and scallions on top.
Drizzle the lemon juice over the eggs and garnish with additional parsley if desired.
Serve the smoky eggs skillet hot, straight from the oven.
Let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Enjoy with your favorite Middle Eastern sides, such as pita bread or olives.
